hero.headlineHtml: India's First Structured Behavioural Training Framework for Drivers.

intro.descriptionHtml:
The KaramSetu Driver Upskilling Framework is built around five core training pillars — each addressing a critical gap in how India's drivers currently operate and how they are perceived.

Together, these five pillars cover everything a driver needs to become a certified, trusted, and professional service provider — beyond simply knowing how to drive.

eligibility.description: KaramSetu is open to all professional drivers across India — taxi drivers, app-based drivers, commercial drivers, household drivers, and government-linked drivers.
requirements: Valid Driving Licence (DL) and relevant vehicle permits
Basic identity verification (Aadhaar and police verification)
Minimum driving experience as defined by your employer or aggregator
Basic mobile usage capability for digital learning access
No major criminal or safety violations on record
Commitment to professional conduct and compliance standards

pillars.title: Pillar 1 — Behaviour & Professional Attitude
points: Understanding the role and responsibility of a professional driver
Punctuality, discipline, and accountability in daily work
Emotional control — handling frustration, pressure, and difficult passengers calmly
Conflict resolution and de-escalation techniques
Respectful conduct in public spaces and with diverse passengers
Attitude towards women, senior citizens, and children

pillars.title: Pillar 2 — Communication Skills
points: Polite and professional greeting etiquette at the start of every trip
How to communicate route changes, delays, or issues clearly
Handling passenger complaints without escalation
Language sensitivity — tone, volume, and choice of words
Basic multilingual communication for diverse passenger interactions
Digital communication — responding to platform messages professionally

pillars.title: Pillar 3 — Grooming & Personal Hygiene
points: Personal grooming standards — dress, hair, and hygiene expectations
Vehicle cleanliness protocols — interior, exterior, and odour management
Daily and weekly hygiene checklists for drivers and their vehicles
Seasonal and context-specific presentation (corporate trips, airport pickups, night rides)
What to do and what to avoid in terms of personal habits during a trip

pillars.title: Pillar 4 — Safety & Security Awareness
points: Women and child safety — conduct, communication, and zero-tolerance standards
Night ride protocols and safe drop practices
Household and private driver security awareness
Emergency response — accidents, medical situations, and helpline coordination
Defensive driving mindset and passenger-first safety culture
Understanding and respecting passenger boundaries

pillars.title: Pillar 5 — Rights & Duties of Drivers
points: Legal rights of drivers under Indian motor vehicle and labour law
Passenger safety obligations — from pickup to safe drop
Documentation and permit compliance — licence, insurance, PUC, fitness
Understanding aggregator compliance and platform policies
Anti-discrimination obligations — equal service regardless of gender, religion, or location
Traffic law awareness — mobile use, substance use, and enhanced penalty education
Grievance handling and fair billing practices

cta.headlineHtml: One Program. A Lifetime of Professional Recognition.
